## context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

attachment.content:
- cc-switch-deep-dive: 诊断 CC Switch、Claude/Codex provider 路由、15721 本地代理、settings.json、statusLine、claude1 菜单和请求打错上游的问题。
- cohort-delivery: Cohort 项目交付引导。分析题目 prompt，拆解为多个 session，逐个生成自然风格英文 prompt 供云端 Claude Code 执行，最终辅助打包验证。

触发条件：
- 用户说 /cohort
- 用户说"拆解题目"、"生成 session prompt"、"打包检查"
- frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- git: Git 快速提交并推送。用户输入 /git <描述> 后自动执行 git add、commit、push 三步操作。

触发条件：
- 用户说 /git
- 用户说 /git commit
- 用户说"快速提交"、"git push"、"git 提交"
- grill-me: Interview the user relentlessly about a plan or design until reaching shared understanding, resolving each branch of the decision tree. Use when user wants to stress-test a plan, get grilled on their design, or mentions "grill me".
- grill-with-docs: Grilling session that challenges your plan against the existing domain model, sharpens terminology, and updates documentation (CONTEXT.md, ADRs) inline as decisions crystallise. Use when user wants to stress-test a plan against their project's language and documented decisions.
- improve-codebase-architecture: Find deepening opportunities in a codebase, informed by the domain language in CONTEXT.md and the decisions in docs/adr/. Use when the user wants to improve architecture, find refactoring opportunities, consolidate tightly-coupled modules, or make a codebase more testable and AI-navigable.
- ml-init: Initialize ML workflow structure in the current project. Creates ml/ directory
with CONTEXT.md, plan.md, log/, and references/ for experiment tracking and
cross-session knowledge persistence.
- modal-ml: 在 Mac 上用 Modal（modal.com）跑 ML 实验的工作流与规范：本地零依赖编排、全程远端执行、
上传前预检、远端执行确认、Image/Volume/GPU/Secret 配置、费用控制、版本坑规避。

只要任务涉及以下任一项就激活本 skill（即使用户没说"用 skill"）：
- 提到 Modal / modal.com / modal run / modal deploy / modal.App / modal.Image / modal.Volume
- 提到在云端跑 GPU、租 GPU、远端训练/微调/推理/批量评估
- 写或改 train.py 之类要发到 Modal 远端执行的脚本
- 出现"上传了但远端没跑成功""本地能跑远端崩""smoke test""checkpoint 持久化"等 Modal 相关困扰
- 选 GPU 型号、配 Image 依赖、挂数据集、管 Secret、控制 Modal 费用
- tdd: Test-driven development with red-green-refactor loop. Use when user wants to build features or fix bugs using TDD, mentions "red-green-refactor", wants integration tests, or asks for test-first development.
- to-issues: Break a plan, spec, or PRD into independently-grabbable issues on the project issue tracker using tracer-bullet vertical slices. Use when user wants to convert a plan into issues, create implementation tickets, or break down work into issues.
- write-a-prd: Turn the current conversation context into a PRD and publish it to the project issue tracker. Use when user wants to create a PRD from the current context.
- writing: 把中文正式文档写出真人质感的写作模式：先用边界优先工作流锁住"写什么、哪些是真的、哪些已完成"，再用六条写作手艺把字写出节奏和具体，最后用三层反模式表做收尾 lint。
重点是写得好（有手艺、有质感），不只是删 AI 腔；目标是写得准确得体，不像 AI 是副产品——不是降 AIGC 检测，不是骗检测器。
凡起草/修改/润色 计划书、申报书、项目介绍、报告、论文、答辩材料、对外正式文档，或用户说"去AI味/降AI腔/这段太AI了/读着像模板/写得像人一点/帮我把这段改改"，都用本 skill——即使没明说"写作模式"。
- wuying-tunnel: wuying-tunnel
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.thinking:
yufeng 想做一个桌面备忘录小组件，类似于一直挂在桌面上的便签/备忘录应用，而且要"可爱"的风格。

这是 macOS 平台，需要了解一下 yufeng 的具体需求再规划。

让我先想想技术方案：
1. macOS 原生 SwiftUI 桌面小组件（Widget）
2. Electron 应用 - 做一个透明窗口钉在桌面
3. Python + tkinter/PyQt 做一个桌面便签
4. Web 技术 + Tauri
5. 简单的 HTML + CSS + JS，用 Electron 包装

考虑到 yufeng 的技术栈是 FastAPI+Vue3+Agent，代码能力偏弱，我觉得 Electron + Vue3 会是最合适的方案，因为他已经熟悉 Vue3 了。不过在规划之前，我需要先确认他的具体需求。

content.text: 有意思！在开始规划之前，我需要先确认几个关键点。

questions.question: 你想要的备忘录大概是什么样的？是类似便利贴/便签那种小窗口钉在桌面上，还是像 macOS 原生 Widget 那样嵌入桌面？

options.label: 便签小窗口
options.description: 一个半透明/可爱风格的小窗口，始终置顶显示在桌面上，可以拖动位置

options.label: 桌面嵌入式
options.description: 像 macOS Widget 一样嵌入桌面壁纸层，不遮挡其他窗口

options.label: 菜单栏挂件
options.description: 点击菜单栏图标弹出备忘录面板

questions.question: 功能上你需要哪些？

options.label: 纯文本备忘
options.description: 简单的文本输入、保存、展示，够用就行

options.label: 待办清单
options.description: 可以添加/勾选/删除待办事项（checkbox 风格）

options.label: 两者都要
options.description: 既能写备忘文字，也能管理待办清单

questions.question: 技术栈你有偏好吗？你熟悉 Vue3，用 Electron+Vue3 做比较顺手，或者你想尝试别的？

options.label: Electron + Vue3
options.description: 你最熟悉的栈，开发快，但打包后体积偏大（~150MB）

options.label: Tauri + Vue3
options.description: 更轻量（~10MB），性能好，但需要 Rust 环境

options.label: Python + PyQt
options.description: Python 桌面应用，轻量，但 UI 定制自由度稍低

options.label: 你来推荐
options.description: 让我根据需求推荐最合适的方案

yufeng 的选择：
1. 便签小窗口 - 半透明/可爱风格，始终置顶显示

input.query: create new Tauri project with npm, prerequisites, scaffold project setup with frontend framework Vue

content.text:
### Create Tauri App with npm

Source: https://v2.tauri.app/_fragments/cta

Initialize a new Tauri project using the npm package manager.

```npm
npm create tauri-app@latest
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize a New Tauri Application

Source: https://v2.tauri.app/learn/sidecar-nodejs

Commands to create a new Tauri project using various shell environments and package managers. Choose the command that matches your preferred development setup.

```sh
sh <(curl https://create.tauri.app/sh)
```

```powershell
irm https://create.tauri.app/ps | iex
```

```fish
sh (curl -sSL https://create.tauri.app/sh | psub)
```

```npm
npm create tauri-app@latest
```

```yarn
yarn create tauri-app
```

```pnpm
pnpm create tauri-app
```

```deno
deno run -A npm:create-tauri-app
```

```bun
bun create tauri-app
```

```cargo
cargo install create-tauri-app --locked
cargo create-tauri-app
```

--------------------------------

### Interactive Tauri Project Setup with pnpm

Source: https://v2.tauri.app/learn/security/using-plugin-permissions

Complete interactive setup process for creating a Tauri application named 'plugin-permission-demo' with TypeScript frontend, pnpm package manager, and Vanilla UI template.

```bash
pnpm create tauri-app

✔ Project name · plugin-permission-demo
✔ Choose which language to use for your frontend · TypeScript / JavaScript - (pnpm, yarn, npm, bun)
✔ Choose your package manager · pnpm
✔ Choose your UI template · Vanilla
✔ Choose your UI flavor · TypeScript

Template created! To get started run:
cd plugin-permission-demo
pnpm install
pnpm tauri dev
```

### Create a Project

Source: https://v2.tauri.app/start/create-project

Tauri is designed to be flexible and compatible with virtually any frontend framework. The `create-tauri-app` utility simplifies the setup process by providing officially maintained templates for popular frameworks like React, Vue, Svelte, and more. Users can also utilize community-contributed templates or add Tauri to an existing codebase.

--------------------------------

### Create a Project > Using create-tauri-app > Scaffold a new project

Source: https://v2.tauri.app/start/create-project

During the scaffolding process, you will be asked to provide a unique bundle identifier for your application. The utility supports multiple frontend languages including Rust, TypeScript/JavaScript, and .NET. Depending on the chosen language, different UI templates and flavors such as Blazor, Yew, or React will be available for selection.

# 桌面便签备忘录 - 实现计划

## Context

yufeng 想做一个始终置顶在桌面的可爱便签小工具，橘子风格，支持备忘文字和待办清单。要求轻巧可爱，不要复杂设计。技术栈选定 Tauri v2 + Vue3（打包体积约 10MB，远小于 Electron）。

## 项目概览

- **项目名**: `cute-sticky` (或 yufeng 自定义)
- **位置**: `/Users/admin/Desktop/cute-sticky`
- **核心体验**: 一个圆润的小窗口钉在桌面上，像一颗橘子一样可爱，随时记录和查看

## 技术方案

### 1. 项目初始化

```bash
cd ~/Desktop
npm create tauri-app@latest cute-sticky -- --template vue-ts
cd cute-sticky
npm install
npm install @tauri-apps/plugin-store
```

Rust 侧添加 store 插件依赖：
```bash
cd src-tauri
cargo add tauri-plugin-store
```

### 2. Tauri 窗口配置 (`src-tauri/tauri.conf.json`)

关键窗口属性：
- `decorations: false` — 去掉原生标题栏，自己画
- `alwaysOnTop: true` — 始终置顶
- `transparent: true` — 背景透明（圆角效果需要）
- `width: 320, height: 420` — 小巧尺寸
- `resizable: true` — 允许调整大小
- `skipTaskbar: true` — 不占任务栏位置

### 3. 前端 UI 设计（Vue3 单页面，极简结构）

整体只有 **3 个组件**，保持轻巧：

```
src/
  App.vue            — 主容器 + 拖拽标题栏 + 颜色切换
  components/
    MemoArea.vue     — 备忘文字区域（textarea）
    TodoList.vue     — 待办清单（checkbox 列表 + 输入框）
```

#### App.vue 布局
```
┌──────────────────────┐
│  [橘子icon] 备忘录  ─ ×│  ← 自定义标题栏（可拖拽）
├──────────────────────┤
│  [备忘] [待办]  切换Tab │  ← 两个 tab 切换
├──────────────────────┤
│                      │
│   内容区域            │  ← MemoArea 或 TodoList
│                      │
├──────────────────────┤
│  🟠🟢🔵🟣  颜色选择   │  ← 底部颜色圆点
└──────────────────────┘
```

#### 橘子风格设计要点
- **圆角**: `border-radius: 20px`，整体圆润
- **默认配色**: 橘子橙 `#FF9843` 标题栏 + 奶白 `#FFF8F0` 内容区
- **字体**: 系统圆体字（macOS 自带 `.AppleSystemUIFontRounded`）
- **阴影**: 柔和投影 `box-shadow: 0 8px 32px rgba(255,152,67,0.3)`
- **标题栏橘子图标**: 用 CSS/emoji 实现，不引入图标库

#### 可选颜色预设（4-5 个）
| 名称 | 标题栏色 | 内容区色 |
|------|----------|----------|
| 橘子 | #FF9843 | #FFF8F0 |
| 薄荷 | #5CC8A8 | #F0FFF8 |
| 蓝莓 | #6C9BCF | #F0F5FF |
| 葡萄 | #B07CC6 | #F8F0FF |
| 草莓 | #FF6B81 | #FFF0F3 |

### 4. 功能实现

#### 备忘文字 (MemoArea.vue)
- 一个 `<textarea>`，自动保存（debounce 500ms）
- 纯文本，不搞富文本编辑器
- placeholder: "随便写点什么..."

#### 待办清单 (TodoList.vue)
- 数据结构: `{id, text, done, createdAt}`
- 底部输入框 + 回车添加
- 点击 checkbox 勾选/取消
- 已完成项划线 + 透明度降低
- 左滑或点击删除按钮移除

#### 数据持久化
- 使用 `@tauri-apps/plugin-store` 存到本地 JSON 文件
- 存储内容：memo 文本、todo 列表、选中的颜色主题
- 自动保存，无需手动点保存按钮

### 5. 窗口交互

- **拖拽移动**: 标题栏区域添加 `data-tauri-drag-region` 属性即可
- **关闭按钮**: 调用 `getCurrentWindow().hide()` 隐藏到托盘，而非退出
- **系统托盘**: 点击托盘图标切换显示/隐藏（可选，第一版可以先不做）

## 实现步骤

1. **脚手架搭建**: `npm create tauri-app@latest`，选 Vue + TypeScript
2. **配置窗口**: 修改 `tauri.conf.json`，设置无边框、置顶、透明、尺寸
3. **Rust 侧**: `main.rs` / `lib.rs` 注册 store 插件，配置 capabilities
4. **App.vue**: 实现自定义标题栏 + Tab 切换 + 颜色选择
5. **MemoArea.vue**: textarea + 自动保存
6. **TodoList.vue**: 清单 CRUD + 自动保存
7. **样式打磨**: 圆角、阴影、动画过渡、橘子配色
8. **测试运行**: `npm run tauri dev` 本地调试

## 前置条件

yufeng 需要确保已安装：
- Node.js (>= 18)
- Rust 工具链 (`rustup`)
- Xcode Command Line Tools（macOS 编译需要）

## 验证方式

1. `npm run tauri dev` 启动后，桌面出现圆润的橘色小窗口
2. 窗口始终在最顶层，可拖拽移动
3. 能在备忘和待办之间切换
4. 输入内容后关闭重开，数据仍在
5. 点击底部颜色圆点，整体配色切换

计划写好了。总结一下方案：

**Tauri v2 + Vue3** 做一个桌面便签，核心特点：

- **橘子风格**: 圆角 20px、橘橙色标题栏、奶白内容区、柔和阴影
- **极简结构**: 只有 3 个组件（App + MemoArea + TodoList）

- **5 种颜色主题**: 橘子/薄荷/蓝莓/葡萄/草莓，底部圆点切换
- **窗口**: 无边框、始终置顶、透明背景、可拖拽，小巧 320x420
- **打包体积**: ~10MB，比 Electron 轻 15 倍

## Approved Plan:
# 桌面便签备忘录 - 实现计划

## Context

yufeng 想做一个始终置顶在桌面的可爱便签小工具，橘子风格，支持备忘文字和待办清单。要求轻巧可爱，不要复杂设计。技术栈选定 Tauri v2 + Vue3（打包体积约 10MB，远小于 Electron）。

## 项目概览

- **项目名**: `cute-sticky` (或 yufeng 自定义)
- **位置**: `/Users/admin/Desktop/cute-sticky`
- **核心体验**: 一个圆润的小窗口钉在桌面上，像一颗橘子一样可爱，随时记录和查看

## 技术方案

### 1. 项目初始化

```bash
cd ~/Desktop
npm create tauri-app@latest cute-sticky -- --template vue-ts
cd cute-sticky
npm install
npm install @tauri-apps/plugin-store
```

Rust 侧添加 store 插件依赖：
```bash
cd src-tauri
cargo add tauri-plugin-store
```

### 2. Tauri 窗口配置 (`src-tauri/tauri.conf.json`)

关键窗口属性：
- `decorations: false` — 去掉原生标题栏，自己画
- `alwaysOnTop: true` — 始终置顶
- `transparent: true` — 背景透明（圆角效果需要）
- `width: 320, height: 420` — 小巧尺寸
- `resizable: true` — 允许调整大小
- `skipTaskbar: true` — 不占任务栏位置

### 3. 前端 UI 设计（Vue3 单页面，极简结构）

整体只有 **3 个组件**，保持轻巧：

```
src/
  App.vue            — 主容器 + 拖拽标题栏 + 颜色切换
  components/
    MemoArea.vue     — 备忘文字区域（textarea）
    TodoList.vue     — 待办清单（checkbox 列表 + 输入框）
```

#### App.vue 布局
```
┌──────────────────────┐
│  [橘子icon] 备忘录  ─ ×│  ← 自定义标题栏（可拖拽）
├──────────────────────┤
│  [备忘] [待办]  切换Tab │  ← 两个 tab 切换
├──────────────────────┤
│                      │
│   内容区域            │  ← MemoArea 或 TodoList
│                      │
├──────────────────────┤
│  🟠🟢🔵🟣  颜色选择   │  ← 底部颜色圆点
└──────────────────────┘
```

#### 橘子风格设计要点
- **圆角**: `border-radius: 20px`，整体圆润
- **默认配色**: 橘子橙 `#FF9843` 标题栏 + 奶白 `#FFF8F0` 内容区
- **字体**: 系统圆体字（macOS 自带 `.AppleSystemUIFontRounded`）
- **阴影**: 柔和投影 `box-shadow: 0 8px 32px rgba(255,152,67,0.3)`
- **标题栏橘子图标**: 用 CSS/emoji 实现，不引入图标库

#### 可选颜色预设（4-5 个）
| 名称 | 标题栏色 | 内容区色 |
|------|----------|----------|
| 橘子 | #FF9843 | #FFF8F0 |
| 薄荷 | #5CC8A8 | #F0FFF8 |
| 蓝莓 | #6C9BCF | #F0F5FF |
| 葡萄 | #B07CC6 | #F8F0FF |
| 草莓 | #FF6B81 | #FFF0F3 |

### 4. 功能实现

#### 备忘文字 (MemoArea.vue)
- 一个 `<textarea>`，自动保存（debounce 500ms）
- 纯文本，不搞富文本编辑器
- placeholder: "随便写点什么..."

#### 待办清单 (TodoList.vue)
- 数据结构: `{id, text, done, createdAt}`
- 底部输入框 + 回车添加
- 点击 checkbox 勾选/取消
- 已完成项划线 + 透明度降低
- 左滑或点击删除按钮移除

#### 数据持久化
- 使用 `@tauri-apps/plugin-store` 存到本地 JSON 文件
- 存储内容：memo 文本、todo 列表、选中的颜色主题
- 自动保存，无需手动点保存按钮

### 5. 窗口交互

- **拖拽移动**: 标题栏区域添加 `data-tauri-drag-region` 属性即可
- **关闭按钮**: 调用 `getCurrentWindow().hide()` 隐藏到托盘，而非退出
- **系统托盘**: 点击托盘图标切换显示/隐藏（可选，第一版可以先不做）

## 实现步骤

1. **脚手架搭建**: `npm create tauri-app@latest`，选 Vue + TypeScript
2. **配置窗口**: 修改 `tauri.conf.json`，设置无边框、置顶、透明、尺寸
3. **Rust 侧**: `main.rs` / `lib.rs` 注册 store 插件，配置 capabilities
4. **App.vue**: 实现自定义标题栏 + Tab 切换 + 颜色选择
5. **MemoArea.vue**: textarea + 自动保存
6. **TodoList.vue**: 清单 CRUD + 自动保存
7. **样式打磨**: 圆角、阴影、动画过渡、橘子配色
8. **测试运行**: `npm run tauri dev` 本地调试

## 前置条件

yufeng 需要确保已安装：
- Node.js (>= 18)
- Rust 工具链 (`rustup`)
- Xcode Command Line Tools（macOS 编译需要）

## 验证方式

1. `npm run tauri dev` 启动后，桌面出现圆润的橘色小窗口
2. 窗口始终在最顶层，可拖拽移动
3. 能在备忘和待办之间切换
4. 输入内容后关闭重开，数据仍在
5. 点击底部颜色圆点，整体配色切换

input.content:
<script setup lang="ts">
import { ref, onMounted } from 'vue'
import { load } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-store'

const memo = ref('')
let store: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof load>> | null = null
let saveTimer: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null = null

onMounted(async () => {
  store = await load('sticky-data.json', { autoSave: true })
  const saved = await store.get<string>('memo')
  if (saved) memo.value = saved
})

function onInput() {
  if (saveTimer) clearTimeout(saveTimer)
  saveTimer = setTimeout(async () => {
    await store?.set('memo', memo.value)
  }, 500)
}
</script>

<template>
  <textarea
    v-model="memo"
    class="memo-textarea"
    placeholder="随便写点什么..."
    @input="onInput"
  />
</template>

<style scoped>
.memo-textarea {
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  border: none;
  outline: none;
  resize: none;
  background: transparent;
  font-family: inherit;
  font-size: 14px;
  line-height: 1.7;
  color: #555;
  padding: 4px 0;
  user-select: text;
}

.memo-textarea::placeholder {
  color: #ccc;
}
</style>

content.thinking:
Let me wait for the build to complete. While waiting, let me check if the patch approach works or if I need a different strategy. Actually, [patch.crates-io] with `version` might not work the way I think. The `[patch]` section replaces a crate with another source, but `version = "=3.0.0"` with `crates-io` might just use the same crate from crates.io but at version 3.0.0. The issue is that `brotli` internally depends on `alloc-no-stdlib = ">=2.0"` while `alloc-stdlib` depends on `alloc-no-stdlib = "3.0"`. 

The patch should force both to use 3.0.0, but I'm realizing the syntax might be off. The `[patch]` section in Cargo actually replaces a crate source entirely rather than just forcing a version, so I need to think through the correct approach here.

Looking at the actual dependencies, brotli requires `alloc-no-stdlib >= 2.0, < 3.0`, while `alloc-stdlib 0.2.3` depends on `alloc-no-stdlib = "3.0"`, creating a version conflict that can't be resolved with standard patching.

This is a genuine upstream incompatibility in brotli 8.0.3 where the crate implements traits from two different versions of the same dependency, which Rust treats as distinct types. I need to either downgrade Rust or find an alternative solution.

Let me try pinning brotli to version 7.0 instead, though I'm realizing the patch syntax might not support that approach directly.

Maybe I should look for an older Rust version that doesn't trigger this issue, or search online to see if others have encountered this specific problem.

content.thinking:
The issue is that `brotli 8.0.3` depends on `alloc-no-stdlib ^2.0` directly but also on `alloc-stdlib ~0.2` which depends on `alloc-no-stdlib 3.0`. This version conflict causes the build to fail.

Looking at the crates.io data, `brotli 8.0.3` was published on 2026-05-28, so it's very recent. And brotli 8.0.2 was published on 2025-08-16. Let me check if 8.0.2 has the same issue.
 
I see that `alloc-stdlib` is only pulled in when the `std` feature is enabled (which is the default), so disabling that feature would avoid the conflict. However, since brotli is a transitive dependency of Tauri, I can't easily control its features. I'm considering downgrading brotli to 7.0.0 as a potential solution.

Actually, I realize that `^2.0` means `>= 2.0.0, < 3.0.0`, so upgrading to 3.0.0 won't work—that's why Cargo is resolving to two versions in the first place. The real issue is that brotli needs to update its dependency constraint to `^3.0` or higher. Let me try using the `[patch]` section to override this properly. The real issue is that brotli is trying to use types from both versions of alloc-no-stdlib simultaneously, which violates Rust's coherence rules—this looks like an upstream bug in brotli 8.0.3. Let me try downgrading brotli to 8.0.2 to see if that version resolved the dependencies differently, since 8.0.3 was just released very recently.

content.thinking:
Wait, the output shows "error: could not compile `brotli` (lib) due to 36 previous errors" but the background task says it completed with exit code 0. That's because my command was `cargo build 2>&1 | tail -15` - the `tail` command succeeded (exit code 0) even though cargo build failed.

So brotli 8.0.2 has the same issue. Let me try a different approach. Since the problem is two versions of alloc-no-stdlib (2.0.4 and 3.0.0), I need a more creative solution.
I'll start by trying to pin alloc-stdlib to 0.2.2, which might depend on an older version of alloc-no-stdlib that's compatible with what brotli needs. If that doesn't work, I could try pinning brotli to 7.0.0 instead, which might not have alloc-stdlib as a default feature.

项目已完成。总结一下：

**cute-sticky 桌面便签** 已在 `/Users/admin/Desktop/cute-sticky` 搭建完成并运行。

input.description: Read current App.vue to understand the design

lastPrompt: /grill-me 使用front-desiner进行优化 我认为现在很廉价的干净，橘子就是那种廉价的emoji现在看来

content.text:
看完了现有代码。你说得对 -- 现在标题栏就是一块纯色 + 一个系统 emoji，确实像是随便拼的。

我们逐个决定怎么改。第一个问题：

---

**标题栏的橘子图标，你想要什么风格？**

现在是 `🍊` 系统 emoji，看起来确实廉价。几个方向：

- **A) 纯 CSS 手绘橘子** -- 用 CSS 画一个简笔橘子（圆形 + 小叶子），精致但极轻量，不引入任何外部资源
- **B) 内联 SVG 图标** -- 手写一个小型 SVG 橘子图标嵌入组件中，可以精确控制颜色和细节，比 emoji 精致很多
- **C) 不要图标** -- 直接去掉图标，标题栏只留文字，走极简路线
- **推荐 B)** -- SVG 体积小（<1KB），能跟主题色联动，质感远超 emoji

你选哪个？

content.thinking:
The user wants two things:

1. More refined/polished design (not the cheap emoji look)
2. A new "常用" (Favorites/Links) feature - a third tab that shows frequently used website links with clickable hyperlinks. This tab should be toggleable - when enabled it appears as a third tab, when disabled it's hidden.

Let me continue the grill-me interview, asking about these one at a time.

Let me first address the icon question (they said "足够精致" = sufficiently exquisite), then move to the links feature.

content.text:
明白，要精致 + 新增一个可开关的"常用链接"栏。我们一个一个来。

---

**Q1: 标题栏图标 -- 你说要足够精致，那我推荐用内联 SVG 画一个小橘子（带叶子、有渐变光泽），不引入外部资源。这个方向 OK 吗？还是你有其他想法？**

比如你想要的是某种特定的 icon 风格（像 macOS 系统图标那种半拟物、或者纯线条简笔画）？

content.text:
全部确认。总结一下设计决策，我来写最终计划然后开干：

**确认的改动清单：**

1. **橘子图标** -- 内联 SVG，半拟物渐变风格（高光+阴影+弧形叶子）
2. **新增"常用"Tab** -- 标题栏右侧加一个链接图标按钮，开启后出现第三个 Tab
3. **链接列表** -- 竖向列表，点名字用系统浏览器跳转，hover 显示删除按钮
4. **添加链接** -- 两步式：先输入 URL 回车，再输入显示名称（默认提取域名）
5. **Tab 样式** -- 去掉填充色背景，改为底部滑动指示条 + 平滑过渡动画
6. **底部主题栏** -- hover 底部区域时才淡入显示，圆点更小，选中时微弹动画
7. **字体** -- 全局换为 SF Pro Rounded 系统圆体
8. **整体细节** -- 间距、圆角、阴影、过渡动画全面打磨

我现在开始实现。

input.prompt:
You need to rewrite 4 frontend files for a Tauri v2 + Vue3 desktop sticky note app at `/Users/admin/Desktop/cute-sticky/src/`. The app is a small always-on-top frameless window (320x420) with rounded corners.

## Design Requirements

### Global Style (`src/style.css`)
- Reset + transparent background
- Font: `'SF Pro Rounded', -apple-system, system-ui, sans-serif`
- `user-select: none` globally, but `user-select: text` on inputs/textareas
- Smooth scrollbar (4px, subtle)

### App.vue - Complete Rewrite
The main container with titlebar, tabs, content, and theme bar.

**Titlebar:**
- Left: Inline SVG orange icon (semi-skeuomorphic style with radial gradient highlight, subtle shadow, green leaf on top). About 20x20px. NOT an emoji.
- Center-left: "cute sticky" text, white, font-weight 600, 14px
- Right side buttons (22x22 circles, `rgba(255,255,255,0.25)` bg):
  - Link toggle button (small SVG chain/link icon, white). When active, it glows slightly. This toggles visibility of the "常用" (links) tab.
  - Close button "×"
- Titlebar uses `data-tauri-drag-region` for dragging
- Background: `var(--header-color)`

**Tabs:**
- Show 2 tabs by default: "备忘" and "待办"
- When link toggle is active, show 3rd tab: "常用"
- Style: NO background fill on tabs. Instead, a sliding indicator bar below the active tab
- The indicator bar should be a 3px rounded bar in `var(--header-color)` that smoothly slides via CSS transition when switching tabs
- Tab text: 13px, inactive=#aaa, active=var(--header-color), font-weight 600

**Content area:**
- `flex: 1`, `overflow-y: auto`, `padding: 12px 16px`
- Shows MemoArea, TodoList, or LinkList based on active tab

**Theme bar (bottom):**
- Hidden by default (`opacity: 0`)
- Fades in (`opacity: 1`) when hovering the bottom 40px area of the container
- 5 small dots (12px), with a subtle scale bounce animation on the active one
- Themes array (keep same colors):
  ```
  橘子: header=#FF9843, bg=#FFF8F0
  薄荷: header=#5CC8A8, bg=#F0FFF8
  蓝莓: header=#6C9BCF, bg=#F0F5FF
  葡萄: header=#B07CC6, bg=#F8F0FF
  草莓: header=#FF6B81, bg=#FFF0F3
  ```

**State management:**
- Use `@tauri-apps/plugin-store` with `load('sticky-data.json', { autoSave: true })`
- Persist: themeIndex, activeTab, showLinks (boolean)
- Import components: MemoArea, TodoList, LinkList
- Close window: `getCurrentWindow().close()`

**The SVG orange icon (IMPORTANT - make it beautiful):**
```html
<svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 100 100">
  <!-- Main orange body with radial gradient -->
  <defs>
    <radialGradient id="orangeGrad" cx="40%" cy="35%" r="60%">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#FFD180"/>
      <stop offset="50%" stop-color="#FF9843"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#E8751A"/>
    </radialGradient>
    <radialGradient id="highlight" cx="35%" cy="25%" r="30%">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="rgba(255,255,255,0.6)"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="rgba(255,255,255,0)"/>
    </radialGradient>
  </defs>
  <circle cx="50" cy="55" r="40" fill="url(#orangeGrad)"/>
  <circle cx="50" cy="55" r="40" fill="url(#highlight)"/>
  <!-- Leaf -->
  <path d="M50 18 Q58 8 65 15 Q58 22 50 18Z" fill="#4CAF50"/>
  <path d="M50 18 Q42 10 38 18 Q44 22 50 18Z" fill="#66BB6A"/>
  <!-- Stem -->
  <rect x="48" y="12" width="4" height="10" rx="2" fill="#795548"/>
</svg>
```

**The link toggle SVG icon:**
```html
<svg width="12" height="12" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round">
  <path d="M10 13a5 5 0 0 0 7.54.54l3-3a5 5 0 0 0-7.07-7.07l-1.72 1.71"/>
  <path d="M14 11a5 5 0 0 0-7.54-.54l-3 3a5 5 0 0 0 7.07 7.07l1.71-1.71"/>
</svg>
```

### LinkList.vue - New Component
**Structure:**
- List of saved links, each as a row: name on left, delete "×" on right (hidden until hover)
- Click on name -> opens URL in system browser via `import { open } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-shell'` then `open(url)`
- Two-step add flow:
  1. Bottom input: placeholder "粘贴链接..." - user pastes/types URL, presses Enter
  2. Input changes to: placeholder "给它起个名字..." with default value extracted from URL domain (e.g., "github.com" from "https://github.com/xxx"). User can edit, press Enter to save.
  3. After save, revert to step 1
- Data: `{id, name, url}[]`, persisted via store key "links"
- Style consistent with TodoList (same padding, font-size, border-radius, hover effects)
- Link name color: `var(--header-color)` (passed as CSS variable from parent), with underline on hover

### TodoList.vue - Style Refinement
Keep all existing logic. Only refine styles:
- Checkbox color should use CSS variable `var(--header-color)` instead of hardcoded `#5CC8A8`
- The checkmark circle border on unchecked: `#ddd` -> `rgba(0,0,0,0.15)`
- Delete button hover: use `var(--header-color)` with 0.1 opacity background
- Input border-top: `1px dashed rgba(0,0,0,0.08)` instead of `#eee`
- All transitions: `0.2s ease`

### MemoArea.vue - Style Refinement
Keep all existing logic. Only refine styles:
- font-size: 13.5px
- line-height: 1.8
- color: `#444`
- placeholder color: `rgba(0,0,0,0.2)`

## IMPORTANT NOTES
- Do NOT add comments to the code
- All files are at `/Users/admin/Desktop/cute-sticky/src/`
- Read each file first before writing
- The app uses Tauri v2 APIs:
  - `import { getCurrentWindow } from '@tauri-apps/api/window'`
  - `import { load } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-store'`
  - `import { open } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-shell'`
- CSS variables `--header-color`, `--bg-color`, `--shadow-color` are set on the root container
- Make sure LinkList.vue can access `--header-color` (it's set on the parent .sticky-container)

content.text:
All files are clean and correct.

## Execution Summary

### Task Type
Refactor + New Feature

### Changes Made
| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `/Users/admin/Desktop/cute-sticky/src/style.css` | Rewrote global reset: updated font to SF Pro Rounded, added `user-select: text` on inputs/textareas, added smooth 4px scrollbar styling globally |
| `/Users/admin/Desktop/cute-sticky/src/App.vue` | Complete rewrite: replaced emoji with inline SVG orange icon, added link toggle button with chain SVG icon, tab system now uses sliding indicator bar instead of filled background, theme bar dots shrunk to 12px with bounce animation and hover-to-reveal on bottom 40px zone, added showLinks state with persistence, integrated LinkList component |
| `/Users/admin/Desktop/cute-sticky/src/components/MemoArea.vue` | Style refinement: font-size 13.5px, line-height 1.8, color #444, placeholder color rgba(0,0,0,0.2) |
| `/Users/admin/Desktop/cute-sticky/src/components/TodoList.vue` | Style refinement: checkbox uses `var(--header-color)` instead of hardcoded #5CC8A8, unchecked border rgba(0,0,0,0.15), delete hover uses `color-mix` with header color, input border-top dashed rgba(0,0,0,0.08), all transitions 0.2s ease |
| `/Users/admin/Desktop/cute-sticky/src/components/LinkList.vue` | New component: two-step add flow (paste URL -> name it), click to open in system browser via `@tauri-apps/plugin-shell`, hover-reveal delete button, link names colored with `var(--header-color)` and underline on hover, data persisted under store key "links" |

### Core Logic
The App.vue now manages a `showLinks` boolean toggled by a titlebar chain-link button, which controls whether the "常用" tab appears. The tab indicator is an absolutely-positioned div that slides via CSS `transform: translateX()` with a cubic-bezier transition, its width dynamically calculated as `(100% - 32px) / tabCount` to account for container padding. The theme bar sits in a 40px zone at the bottom and fades in only when that zone is hovered.

### Validation Result
PASS -- `vue-tsc --noEmit` zero errors, `vite build` successful (31 modules, 2.35s).

| `/Users/admin/Desktop/cute-sticky/src/App.vue` | Complete rewrite: replaced emoji with inline SVG orange icon, added link toggle button with chain SVG icon, tab system now uses sliding indicator bar instead of filled background, theme bar dots shrunk to 12px with bounce animation and hover-to-reveal on bottom 40px zone, added showLinks state with persistence, integrated LinkList component |
| `/Users/admin/Desktop/cute-sticky/src/components/MemoArea.vue` | Style refinement: font-size 13.5px, line-height 1.8, color #444, placeholder color rgba(0,0,0,0.2) |
| `/Users/admin/Desktop/cute-sticky/src/components/TodoList.vue` | Style refinement: checkbox uses `var(--header-color)` instead of hardcoded #5CC8A8, unchecked border rgba(0,0,0,0.15), delete hover uses `color-mix` with header color, input border-top dashed rgba(0,0,0,0.08), all transitions 0.2s ease |
| `/Users/admin/Desktop/cute-sticky/src/components/LinkList.vue` | New component: two-step add flow (paste URL -> name it), click to open in system browser via `@tauri-apps/plugin-shell`, hover-reveal delete button, link names colored with `var(--header-color)` and underline on hover, data persisted under store key "links" |

### Core Logic
The App.vue now manages a `showLinks` boolean toggled by a titlebar chain-link button, which controls whether the "常用" tab appears. The tab indicator is an absolutely-positioned div that slides via CSS `transform: translateX()` with a cubic-bezier transition, its width dynamically calculated as `(100% - 32px) / tabCount` to account for container padding. The theme bar sits in a 40px zone at the bottom and fades in only when that zone is hovered.

PASS -- `vue-tsc --noEmit` zero errors, `vite build` successful (31 modules, 2.35s).

**本次优化内容：**

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   Yufeng wants to build a cute desktop sticky note app that always stays on top of the desktop, inspired by a classmate's setup. Key requirements evolved through the conversation:
   - Always-on-top, frameless, draggable small window (320x420) with rounded corners
   - Orange/橘子 theme by default, with 5 switchable color themes
   - Three features: text memo, todo checklist, and bookmarkable website links ("常用")
   - The links tab is toggleable via a titlebar button - hidden when not needed
   - Design must be polished and cute, NOT cheap/generic
   - Tech stack: Tauri v2 + Vue3 + TypeScript (lightweight ~10MB bundle)
   - Data persists locally across app restarts

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Tauri v2 desktop app framework (Rust backend + web frontend)
   - Vue 3 Composition API with `<script setup lang="ts">`
   - `@tauri-apps/plugin-store` for local JSON persistence
   - `@tauri-apps/plugin-shell` for opening URLs in system browser
   - Frameless transparent window with `data-tauri-drag-region` for dragging
   - macOS Private API required for transparent windows
   - CSS custom properties (variables) for dynamic theming
   - Inline SVG for polished icons (semi-skeuomorphic orange, chain-link icon)
   - CSS sliding indicator bar for tab switching animation
   - Hover-to-reveal pattern for theme dots and delete buttons
   - Rust dependency version conflicts (brotli/alloc-no-stdlib)

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`/Users/admin/Desktop/cute-sticky/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json`**
     - Core Tauri configuration for window behavior and build settings
     - Window: 320x420, decorations:false, alwaysOnTop:true, transparent:true, skipTaskbar:true
     - `"macOSPrivateApi": true` under app section (required for transparency on macOS)
     - identifier: "com.cute.sticky"

   - **`/Users/admin/Desktop/cute-sticky/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`**
     - Rust entry point registering all Tauri plugins
     ```rust
     #[cfg_attr(mobile, tauri::mobile_entry_point)]
     pub fn run() {
         tauri::Builder::default()
             .plugin(tauri_plugin_store::Builder::new().build())
             .plugin(tauri_plugin_shell::init())
             .setup(|app| {
                 if cfg!(debug_assertions) {
                     app.handle().plugin(
                         tauri_plugin_log::Builder::default()
                             .level(log::LevelFilter::Info)
                             .build(),
                     )?;
                 }
                 Ok(())
             })
             .run(tauri::generate_context!())
             .expect("error while running tauri application");
     }
     ```

   - **`/Users/admin/Desktop/cute-sticky/src-tauri/Cargo.toml`**
     - Rust dependencies; tauri has `features = ["macos-private-api"]` (auto-added by tauri-build)
     - Dependencies: tauri 2.11.2, tauri-plugin-store 2.4.3, tauri-plugin-shell 2.3.5, tauri-plugin-log 2
     - CRITICAL: After any dependency changes, must run `cargo update -p alloc-stdlib --precise 0.2.2` to avoid brotli compilation failure

   - **`/Users/admin/Desktop/cute-sticky/src-tauri/capabilities/default.json`**
     - Tauri permission capabilities
     ```json
     {
       "permissions": [
         "core:default",
         "core:window:allow-start-dragging",
         "core:window:allow-close",
         "core:window:allow-hide",
         "core:window:allow-set-always-on-top",
         "store:default",
         "shell:allow-open"
       ]
     }
     ```

   - **`/Users/admin/Desktop/cute-sticky/src/App.vue`** (fully rewritten in redesign)
     - Main container with: SVG orange icon (radial gradient, leaf, stem), link toggle button (chain SVG), sliding tab indicator, hover-reveal theme bar
     - State: activeTab (memo/todo/links), themeIndex, showLinks boolean - all persisted
     - 5 themes with header/bg/shadow colors
     - Tab indicator uses `transform: translateX()` with cubic-bezier transition
     - Theme bar: opacity 0 by default, fades in on hover of bottom 40px zone
     - Uses `getCurrentWindow().close()` for close button

   - **`/Users/admin/Desktop/cute-sticky/src/components/LinkList.vue`** (new)
     - Two-step add flow: paste URL -> input name (defaults to domain extraction)
     - Opens links via `import { open } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-shell'`
     - Data structure: `{id, name, url}[]`, persisted under store key "links"
     - Link names colored with `var(--header-color)`, underline on hover
     - Hover-reveal delete button

   - **`/Users/admin/Desktop/cute-sticky/src/components/TodoList.vue`** (refined styles)
     - Checkbox uses `var(--header-color)` instead of hardcoded #5CC8A8
     - Delete hover uses `color-mix(in srgb, var(--header-color) 10%, transparent)`
     - Unchecked border: `rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.15)`, transitions: `0.2s ease`

   - **`/Users/admin/Desktop/cute-sticky/src/components/MemoArea.vue`** (refined styles)
     - font-size: 13.5px, line-height: 1.8, color: #444, placeholder: rgba(0,0,0,0.2)

   - **`/Users/admin/Desktop/cute-sticky/src/style.css`** (rewritten)
     - Font: `'SF Pro Rounded', -apple-system, system-ui, sans-serif`
     - Global scrollbar: 4px width, subtle colors
     - `user-select: text` on inputs/textareas

   - **`/Users/admin/Desktop/cute-sticky/src/main.ts`** - Standard Vue mount, unchanged

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **Rust not installed**: User ran interactive curl command which failed in non-TTY. Fixed by adding `-y` flag: `curl ... | sh -s -- -y`
   - **`npm create tauri-app` failed**: Interactive CLI can't run in non-TTY. Fixed by creating Vite project first then running `npx tauri init` with all flags
   - **brotli v8.0.3 compilation error**: `alloc-no-stdlib` had two versions (2.0.4 and 3.0.0) in dependency graph causing trait coherence errors. Tried: cargo update, [patch.crates-io], pinning brotli to 8.0.2 - all failed. **Solution**: `cargo update -p alloc-stdlib --precise 0.2.2` which removed alloc-no-stdlib v3.0.0 entirely
   - **Transparent window warning**: "The window is set to be transparent but the `macos-private-api` is not enabled." Fixed by adding `"macOSPrivateApi": true` to tauri.conf.json under app section. Tauri-build then auto-added `features = ["macos-private-api"]` to Cargo.toml
   - **User feedback on design quality**: User said the design was "廉价的干净" (cheap clean) and the emoji 🍊 looked cheap. This triggered a complete UI redesign with semi-skeuomorphic SVG icons, sliding tab indicators, hover-reveal patterns, and SF Pro Rounded font

5. Problem Solving:
   - Resolved Rust dependency version conflicts through systematic version pinning
   - Handled macOS-specific transparent window requirements
   - Complete UI redesign from cheap-looking to polished through structured user interview (grill-me skill)
   - All tasks (#1-#7) completed successfully. App builds and runs.

6. All user messages:
   - "是这样，我今天看到同学的电脑桌面做了一个有意思的东西，就是类似备忘录一直自动挂在桌面，一直可以看到，我也想做一个可爱的"
   - Answered questions: 便签小窗口, 两者都要 (memo+todo), Tauri + Vue3
   - "我想要可爱的橘子风格然后可以选择颜色这个要特比轻巧可爱，而不是设计复杂"
   - "帮助安装，然后继续" (regarding Rust installation failure)
   - `/grill-me 使用front-desiner进行优化 我认为现在很廉价的干净，橘子就是那种廉价的emoji现在看来`
   - "我要足够精致的，然后就是想加一个就是我常用的网站能支持就是超链接跳转，然后我也能就是随时看，那个叫做常用是一个就是按钮开启时候会产生第三个栏，然后我需要时候就可以开启显示，不需要就关闭就不会显示"
   - "那就用内联，然后我希望你一次多提几个问，然后是使用Claude code内置的那个栏可选的，我希望以后问和答都这样更加好" (preference for using AskUserQuestion tool, multiple questions at once)
   - Answered design questions: 半拟物渐变 icon, 标题栏小图标 for link toggle, 直接编辑 for link management (with custom note about name display), 系统默认浏览器
   - Answered layout questions: 竖向列表, 两步式 add flow, Tab样式太单调+底部主题栏太粗糙+全部重新设计
   - Answered detail questions: 滑动指示条 for tabs, 精致圆点 for theme bar, 系统圆体 font

7. Pending Tasks:
   - No explicitly pending tasks. All 7 tasks completed. The app is running.
   - User has not yet provided feedback on the redesigned UI.

8. Current Work:
   The complete UI redesign was just implemented and the app was restarted. All frontend files were rewritten by a coder agent:
   - App.vue: SVG orange icon, link toggle, sliding tab indicator, hover-reveal theme bar
   - LinkList.vue: New component for bookmarkable links
   - TodoList.vue and MemoArea.vue: Style refinements
   - style.css: SF Pro Rounded font, scrollbar styling
   
   The Tauri dev server was restarted and the app launched successfully (confirmed via process check, no compilation errors). The app is currently running and waiting for user feedback on the new design.

9. Optional Next Step:
   Wait for yufeng's feedback on the redesigned UI. The user may want further visual tweaks, or may identify issues with the new link feature, tab animations, or theme bar behavior. No action should be taken until the user responds with their assessment of the current state.

If you need specific details from before compaction (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/admin/.claude/projects/-Users-admin-Desktop/bd282509-c6ca-4b2e-ad2d-3d9cd0bb8be5.jsonl

# 委派路由

> Agent 路由表 + 风险门控 + ML 工作流。

## 路由表

| 目标 | 对象 | 去向 |
|------|------|------|
| 验证/质疑 | 需求前提 | 主对话 |
| 理解/搜索 | 本地代码/搜索 | Explore |
| 审查 | 本地代码 | reviewer |
| 安全审查 | 本地代码 | security-checker |
| 代码简化 | 本地代码 | code-simplifier |
| 修复/创建/重构 | 单文件 | 主对话 |
| 修复/创建/重构 | 多文件 | coder |
| 对抗审查 | 多文件改动后 | `/codex:adversarial-review` |

### 实验纪律
- 跑前检查：重复检测 + 连续失败计数(>=3 停下换方向) + 假设清晰
- 日志格式：假设/配置/结果/问题/下一步
- GPU 训练通过 Modal 执行，modal-ml skill 自动激活

## 编码完成后流程
多文件改动后：reviewer -> `/codex:adversarial-review` -> 修复 [P1] -> test -> code-simplifier。
跳过：单文件<10行 / .md / .d.ts。

[package]
name = "app"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "A Tauri App"
authors = ["you"]
license = ""
repository = ""
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.77.2"

# See more keys and their definitions at https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/manifest.html

[lib]
name = "app_lib"
crate-type = ["staticlib", "cdylib", "rlib"]

[build-dependencies]
tauri-build = { version = "2.6.2", features = [] }

[dependencies]
serde_json = "1.0"
serde = { version = "1.0", features = ["derive"] }
log = "0.4"

tauri-plugin-log = "2"

attachment.planContent:
# 桌面便签备忘录 - 实现计划

## Context

yufeng 想做一个始终置顶在桌面的可爱便签小工具，橘子风格，支持备忘文字和待办清单。要求轻巧可爱，不要复杂设计。技术栈选定 Tauri v2 + Vue3（打包体积约 10MB，远小于 Electron）。

## 项目概览

- **项目名**: `cute-sticky` (或 yufeng 自定义)
- **位置**: `/Users/admin/Desktop/cute-sticky`
- **核心体验**: 一个圆润的小窗口钉在桌面上，像一颗橘子一样可爱，随时记录和查看

## 技术方案

### 1. 项目初始化

```bash
cd ~/Desktop
npm create tauri-app@latest cute-sticky -- --template vue-ts
cd cute-sticky
npm install
npm install @tauri-apps/plugin-store
```

Rust 侧添加 store 插件依赖：
```bash
cd src-tauri
cargo add tauri-plugin-store
```

### 2. Tauri 窗口配置 (`src-tauri/tauri.conf.json`)

关键窗口属性：
- `decorations: false` — 去掉原生标题栏，自己画
- `alwaysOnTop: true` — 始终置顶
- `transparent: true` — 背景透明（圆角效果需要）
- `width: 320, height: 420` — 小巧尺寸
- `resizable: true` — 允许调整大小
- `skipTaskbar: true` — 不占任务栏位置

### 3. 前端 UI 设计（Vue3 单页面，极简结构）

整体只有 **3 个组件**，保持轻巧：

```
src/
  App.vue            — 主容器 + 拖拽标题栏 + 颜色切换
  components/
    MemoArea.vue     — 备忘文字区域（textarea）
    TodoList.vue     — 待办清单（checkbox 列表 + 输入框）
```

#### App.vue 布局
```
┌──────────────────────┐
│  [橘子icon] 备忘录  ─ ×│  ← 自定义标题栏（可拖拽）
├──────────────────────┤
│  [备忘] [待办]  切换Tab │  ← 两个 tab 切换
├──────────────────────┤
│                      │
│   内容区域            │  ← MemoArea 或 TodoList
│                      │
├──────────────────────┤
│  🟠🟢🔵🟣  颜色选择   │  ← 底部颜色圆点
└──────────────────────┘
```

#### 橘子风格设计要点
- **圆角**: `border-radius: 20px`，整体圆润
- **默认配色**: 橘子橙 `#FF9843` 标题栏 + 奶白 `#FFF8F0` 内容区
- **字体**: 系统圆体字（macOS 自带 `.AppleSystemUIFontRounded`）
- **阴影**: 柔和投影 `box-shadow: 0 8px 32px rgba(255,152,67,0.3)`
- **标题栏橘子图标**: 用 CSS/emoji 实现，不引入图标库

#### 可选颜色预设（4-5 个）
| 名称 | 标题栏色 | 内容区色 |
|------|----------|----------|
| 橘子 | #FF9843 | #FFF8F0 |
| 薄荷 | #5CC8A8 | #F0FFF8 |
| 蓝莓 | #6C9BCF | #F0F5FF |
| 葡萄 | #B07CC6 | #F8F0FF |
| 草莓 | #FF6B81 | #FFF0F3 |

### 4. 功能实现

#### 备忘文字 (MemoArea.vue)
- 一个 `<textarea>`，自动保存（debounce 500ms）
- 纯文本，不搞富文本编辑器
- placeholder: "随便写点什么..."

#### 待办清单 (TodoList.vue)
- 数据结构: `{id, text, done, createdAt}`
- 底部输入框 + 回车添加
- 点击 checkbox 勾选/取消
- 已完成项划线 + 透明度降低
- 左滑或点击删除按钮移除

#### 数据持久化
- 使用 `@tauri-apps/plugin-store` 存到本地 JSON 文件
- 存储内容：memo 文本、todo 列表、选中的颜色主题
- 自动保存，无需手动点保存按钮

### 5. 窗口交互

- **拖拽移动**: 标题栏区域添加 `data-tauri-drag-region` 属性即可
- **关闭按钮**: 调用 `getCurrentWindow().hide()` 隐藏到托盘，而非退出
- **系统托盘**: 点击托盘图标切换显示/隐藏（可选，第一版可以先不做）

## 实现步骤

1. **脚手架搭建**: `npm create tauri-app@latest`，选 Vue + TypeScript
2. **配置窗口**: 修改 `tauri.conf.json`，设置无边框、置顶、透明、尺寸
3. **Rust 侧**: `main.rs` / `lib.rs` 注册 store 插件，配置 capabilities
4. **App.vue**: 实现自定义标题栏 + Tab 切换 + 颜色选择
5. **MemoArea.vue**: textarea + 自动保存
6. **TodoList.vue**: 清单 CRUD + 自动保存
7. **样式打磨**: 圆角、阴影、动画过渡、橘子配色
8. **测试运行**: `npm run tauri dev` 本地调试

## 前置条件

yufeng 需要确保已安装：
- Node.js (>= 18)
- Rust 工具链 (`rustup`)
- Xcode Command Line Tools（macOS 编译需要）

## 验证方式

1. `npm run tauri dev` 启动后，桌面出现圆润的橘色小窗口
2. 窗口始终在最顶层，可拖拽移动
3. 能在备忘和待办之间切换
4. 输入内容后关闭重开，数据仍在
5. 点击底部颜色圆点，整体配色切换

- tdd: Test-driven development with red-green-refactor loop. Use when user wants to build features or fix bugs using TDD, mentions "red-green-refactor", wants integration tests, or asks for test-first development.
- to-issues: Break a plan, spec, or PRD into independently-grabbable issues on the project issue tracker using tracer-bullet vertical slices. Use when user wants to convert a plan into issues, create implementation tickets, or break down work into issues.
- write-a-prd: Turn the current conversation context into a PRD and publish it to the project issue tracker. Use when user wants to create a PRD from the current context.
- writing: 把中文正式文档写出真人质感的写作模式：先用边界优先工作流锁住"写什么、哪些是真的、哪些已完成"，再用六条写作手艺把字写出节奏和具体，最后用三层反模式表做收尾 lint。
重点是写得好（有手艺、有质感），不只是删 AI 腔；目标是写得准确得体，不像 AI 是副产品——不是降 AIGC 检测，不是骗检测器。
凡起草/修改/润色 计划书、申报书、项目介绍、报告、论文、答辩材料、对外正式文档，或用户说"去AI味/降AI腔/这段太AI了/读着像模板/写得像人一点/帮我把这段改改"，都用本 skill——即使没明说"写作模式"。
- wuying-tunnel: wuying-tunnel
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch
